text:
Keep tabs on the latest news out of Haiti and the diaspora delivered straight to your inbox.

|

The Haitian Times

Bridging the gap

Swimmer Alexandre Grand'Pierre, gymnast Lynnzee Brown, Judoka Philippe Metellus and boxer Cedrick Belony-Dulièpre have all competed for Haiti at the 2024 Olympic Games  in Paris but were eliminated in their first outings.

CAP-HAITIEN — Four athletes have competed for Haiti so far at the Olympic Games Paris 2024, swimmer Alexandre Grand’Pierre, gymnast Lynnzee Brown, judoka Philippe Metellus and boxer Cedrick Belony-Dulièpre. Although they won the hearts of scores of Haitians worldwide, all of them were however eliminated in the first round.

Grand’Pierre was the first athlete to represent Haiti in swimming, competing in the 100-meter breaststroke on July 27. Grand’Pierre swam the race in 1:02.85, finishing fourth in his heat and in 28th position overall out of 35 participants. Only the first 16 swimmers qualified for the semi-finals, hence Grand’Pierre was eliminated.

Sign up for our free newsletters.

At just 21 years of age, Grand’Pierre holds Haiti’s record for the 50, 100 and 200-meter breaststroke and 200-meter individual medley. He is the third member of his family to swim at the Olympics for Haiti, following the footsteps of his sisters, Naomy and Emilie Grand’Pierre.

Later on July 29, Brown mesmerized Haitians all over the world with an exquisite vault routine, in which she scored 13.566 points. The Missouri native also dazzled her audience during her floor routine, tallying 12.233 points. In her other routines, Brown racked 11.500 points in the uneven bars and 11.533 points in the beam, earning 48.832 points in total.

Despite a stunning overall performance, Brown did not rack up enough points to move to the next round, finishing in 59th position.

Brown was the first ever gymnast to represent Haiti at the Olympics.

After her performance, judoka Philippe Metellus bowed down to Thailandian Terada Masayuki, 11-0 on Monday, failing to qualify for the next round as well.

Later on Tuesday afternoon, boxer Cedrick Belony-Dulièpre was also defeated in a disappointing scoreline, getting whitewashed by Brazilian Wanderley Pereira 5-0.

Next up for Haiti, swimmer Mayah Chouloute will compete in the 50-meter freestyle and Christopher Borzor will run the 100-meter sprint on Aug. 3. After their performances, Emelia Chatfield will run the 100-meter hurdle on Aug. 7.

As an independent publication, we rely on contributions from readers like you to fund our journalism.

Thanks for your contribution!

Email me atonz@haitiantimes.comOnz Chery is a Haiti correspondent for The Haitian Times. Chery started his journalism career as a City College of New York student with The Campus. He later wrote for First Touch, local soccer leagues in New York and Elite Sports New York before joining The Haitian Times in 2019.

5 Comments

We wholeheartedly congratulate athletes who have qualified to represent Haiti in the current Olympics. That is an immense achievement. Bravo!Competing against the best should serve as a powerful encouragement to persevere, and an indication, perhaps, of the resources needed to take them to the next level of excellency.Henryka ManèsEcoWorks International

I feel so sad for my Haitians behind eliminate from the Olympic in Paris.I can say thanks to them for their performance.Hope next Olympic they come back again and winning.For the rest Haitians athletes in the competions let’ s fighting more hard to get some medals.Show the world we obtained some things.

They need sponsorship and money to win. The best have the best teams. I am grateful for representing Haiti so well. As a community let’s look for opportunities to push our best forward. Well done to all four!

I take pride in my brothers and sisters competing in the Paris Olympics. Looking ahead, I aspire for us to engage in a broader range of events to enhance our medal winning opportunities.

It is an extraordinary accomplishment by itself to be ranked one of the best in the world at any sport or other feat. Much credit to these Haitian athletes who have earned their place among the elite. I am an American but I am always eager to root for my brothers and sisters in Haiti, I hope that a medal will be in your future!

title: Four athletes, four straight elimination for Haiti so far at the Olympics - The Haitian Times
author: Onz Chéry
description: Swimmer Alexandre Grand'Pierre, gymnast Lynnzee Brown, Judoka Philippe Metellus and boxer Cedrick Belony-Dulièpre have all competed for Haiti at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games but were eliminated in their first outings.
